Title: Single-cell RNA sequencing reveals a hierarchical transcriptional regulatory network of terpenoid biosynthesis in cotton secretory glandular cells

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: secretory glandular cells (pigment glands) in leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: specifically expressed in secretory glandular cells; relatively stable or slightly decreased upon pathogen/insect challenge
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates downstream regulators GoHSFA4a and GoNAC42 and is required upstream for terpenoid biosynthesis/gland-associated metabolism
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor associated with pigment gland formation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; ChIP-seq; EMSA; yeast one-hybrid; microscale thermophoresis; dual-LUC transient assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GoPGF directly binds promoters of GoHSFA4a and GoNAC42. 2. Silencing GoPGF decreases GoHSFA4a and GoNAC42 transcripts. 3. GoPGF acts upstream in a hierarchical network controlling gland-associated terpenoid biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core upstream developmental regulator linking gland formation with metabolic regulation; paper emphasizes it as upstream of newly identified TFs rather than as the main newly characterized regulator.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: scRNA-seq: GEO GSE243419; GSA CRA012990; public RNA-seq: SRA PRJNA248163; PRJNA600707
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defense against pathogens and insect herbivores

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: distinctly and remarkably expressed in secretory glandular cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes β-myrcene accumulation and supports formation of heliocides 2 and 3; silencing reduces β-myrcene and heliocides 2/3 and increases trans-β-ocimene, heliocide 1, and heliocide 4
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; metabolite profiling by GC-MS/HPLC; prior enzymatic functional assignment cited
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhTPS6 is SGC-enriched. 2. GhTPS6 catalyzes β-myrcene synthesis from geranyl diphosphate. 3. GhTPS6 supplies diene substrate for Diels-Alder formation of heliocides 2 and 3 in cotton.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Links volatile monoterpene biosynthesis to non-volatile heliocide formation through downstream chemical cycloaddition.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing shifts metabolite profile toward trans-β-ocimene and heliocide 1/4
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts geranyl diphosphate into the monoterpene β-myrcene
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product and supplies substrate for downstream heliocide formation

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: distinctly and remarkably expressed in secretory glandular cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with synthesis of volatile sesquiterpenes β-caryophyllene and α-humulene in SGCs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: scRNA-seq expression localization; prior enzymatic functional assignment cited
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhTPS1 is strongly expressed in SGCs. 2. GhTPS1 synthesizes β-caryophyllene and α-humulene from MVA-derived precursor. 3. It contributes to SGC-specific volatile sesquiterpene production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional assignment referenced from prior work rather than newly revalidated here.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts farnesyl diphosphate into the volatile sesquiterpenes β-caryophyllene and α-humulene

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: distinctly and remarkably expressed in secretory glandular cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with guaia-1(10),11-diene production in SGCs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: scRNA-seq expression localization; prior enzymatic functional assignment cited
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhTPS2 is enriched in SGCs. 2. GhTPS2 primarily forms guaia-1(10),11-diene. 3. It contributes to SGC-specific volatile terpenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly functionally perturbed in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: distinctly and remarkably expressed in secretory glandular cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with volatile monoterpene biosynthesis including L-pinene and β-pinene in SGCs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: scRNA-seq expression localization; prior enzymatic functional assignment cited
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhTPS3 is enriched in SGCs. 2. GhTPS3 is implicated in synthesis of L-pinene and β-pinene. 3. It contributes to SGC-specific monoterpene production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional assignment referenced from prior work rather than newly tested here.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts geranyl diphosphate into monoterpenes including L-pinene and β-pinene

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: gossypol-type non-volatile terpenoids; β-caryophyllene; α-humulene; L-pinene; β-pinene; β-myrcene; trans-β-ocimene
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defense against pathogens and insect herbivores
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: pathogen invasion; insect infestation; developmental signals
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highly enriched in secretory glandular cells/pigment glands; induced by pathogen and insect invasion; minimal expression in glandless CCRI12gl leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates terpenoid biosynthetic genes and promotes both non-volatile gossypol-type terpenoids and volatile terpene accumulation without altering gland development
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: scRNA-seq: GEO GSE243419; GSA CRA012990; multi-tissue RNA-seq: SRA PRJNA248163; PRJNA600707
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key downstream activator of gland-associated terpenoid production responsive to stress and development
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter in pGoHSFA4a::GUS; target promoters assayed by dual-LUC
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of MVA/gossypol-pathway terpenoids and coordination of volatile/non-volatile terpene production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; GUS reporter; subcellular localization; dual-LUC; EMSA; yeast one-hybrid; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GoHSFA4a is specifically expressed in cotton secretory glandular cells. 2. Silencing or CRISPR mutagenesis reduces terpenoid accumulation but not gland development. 3. GoHSFA4a directly binds and activates promoters of HMGS and CYP736A196 and broadly promotes both volatile and non-volatile terpenoid biosynthesis. 4. GoPGF directly activates GoHSFA4a.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite HSFA4 family association with heat response, heat stress did not affect GoHSFA4a expression in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: HMGS; CYP736A196; related MVA and gossypol pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates MVA and gossypol pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: GoPGF; pathogen invasion; insect infestation; developmental signals

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: gossypol-type non-volatile terpenoids; β-caryophyllene; α-humulene; L-pinene; β-pinene; β-myrcene; trans-β-ocimene
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defense against pathogens and insect herbivores
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: pathogen invasion; insect infestation; developmental signals
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highly enriched in secretory glandular cells/pigment glands; induced by pathogen and insect invasion; minimal expression in glandless CCRI12gl leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates terpenoid biosynthetic genes and promotes both non-volatile gossypol-type terpenoids and volatile terpene accumulation without altering gland development
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: scRNA-seq: GEO GSE243419; GSA CRA012990; multi-tissue RNA-seq: SRA PRJNA248163; PRJNA600707
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key downstream activator coordinating gland-specific terpenoid biosynthesis in response to stress and development
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC transcription factor; homolog of AtNAC042
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter in pGoNAC42::GUS; target promoters assayed by dual-LUC
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of MVA/gossypol-pathway terpenoids and coordination of volatile/non-volatile terpene production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; GUS reporter; subcellular localization; dual-LUC; EMSA; yeast one-hybrid; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: No reciprocal transcriptional effect detected between GoNAC42 and GoHSFA4a
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GoNAC42 is specifically expressed in cotton secretory glandular cells. 2. VIGS of GoNAC42 lowers volatile and non-volatile terpenoid levels but does not affect gland development. 3. GoNAC42 directly binds and activates promoters of HMGR and DH1 and broadly activates terpenoid biosynthesis genes. 4. GoPGF directly activates GoNAC42.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts in parallel with GoHSFA4a downstream of GoPGF; no evidence of hierarchical regulation between GoNAC42 and GoHSFA4a.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: HMGR; DH1; related MVA and gossypol pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates MVA and gossypol pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: GoPGF; pathogen invasion; insect infestation; developmental signals

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CGP1 is cited as a known regulator involved in gland pigmentation. 2. It is not a primary experimental focus in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Context gene only; included because explicitly highlighted among SGC TFs, but not functionally characterized here.
